scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

RAM KUMAR,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

SURESH PATEL,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF RS. 15,00,000/- WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

UNDER SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit as the parties are residents of Bengaluru, Karnataka, and the transaction/communications pleaded herein relate to Bengaluru.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

5. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant by NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). (A copy of the NEFT/transaction proof is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A.)

7. There was no written agreement between the parties in respect of the said hand loan.

8. The Defendant acknowledged the loan/receipt of the said amount through WhatsApp messages. (A copy of the WhatsApp messages is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B.)

9. Despite repeated oral demands, the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount. The Plaintiff treats 20.06.2024 as the date of default for the purpose of claiming interest.

10. The Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the said loan amount. (A copy of the legal notice is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C.)

11. The Defendant has not repaid the loan amount even after the said demands.

LEGAL BASIS

12. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is bound to repay the hand loan received from the Plaintiff, and the Defendant’s acknowledgment of the loan (including through WhatsApp messages) supports the Plaintiff’s claim for recovery of the principal sum along with interest as prayed.

CAUSE OF ACTION

13. The cause of action for the present suit arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite oral demands, giving rise to the Plaintiff’s right to sue for recovery.

14. The cause of action continues thereafter on account of non-payment.

LIMITATION

15. The present suit is filed within limitation as it is filed within three years from 20.06.2024 (the date of default pleaded by the Plaintiff).

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

16. The suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- is paid on this plaint as per the applicable court-fee law.

INTEREST

17. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of filing of this suit;

    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;

    (c) Future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till the date of actual realization.

PRAYER

18. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only);

    (b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum on Rs. 15,00,000/- from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of this suit;

    (c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable during the pendency of this suit;

    (d) Award future interest from the date of decree till realization at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit;

    (e) Award costs of the suit; and

    (f) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon'ble Court deems fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

| Sr. No. | Description | Annexure |
|---------|-------------|----------|
| 1. | NEFT/transaction proof of transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- dated 15.03.2024 (UTR: AXIB20240315123456) | Annexure A |
| 2. | WhatsApp messages acknowledging
